perturbation.original_text: 1. In respect of all Confidential Information in whatever form acquired prior to or during the period of this Agreement by one party (the “Receiving Party”) from the other party (the “Disclosing Party”) relating to the Purpose, the Receiving Party undertakes: - A. Only to use the information for evaluating or preparing quotation in respect and/or evaluation of th e Purpose. B. Only to disclose the information and then only to the extent necessary to those of its employees to whom disclosure is necessary. C. Not to disclose the Confidential Information to any third party or the fact that the Confidential Information is b eing exchanged, except with the expressed authorisation in writing by the Disclosing Party and is obliged to enter into an identical confidentiality agreement with the such third party. D. Not to copy or reduce the Confidential Information to writing or store in any computer readable form except as may be reasonably necessary for the Purpose. E. To return to the Disclosing Party on demand all information which has been supplied to or acquired by it in the form of drawings or written material or other recorded for m including all copies thereof, and to delete all information stored in computer readable form, and the Receiving Party will be responsible for the fulfilment of the above obligation on the part of its employees. CWF0 001 Mutual Non -Disclosure Agreement C.W. Fletcher & Sons Ltd
perturbation.changed_text: 1. In respect of all Confidential Information in whatever form acquired prior to or during the period of this Agreement by one party (the “Receiving Party”) from the other party (the “Disclosing Party”) relating to the Purpose, the Receiving Party undertakes: - A. Only to use the information for evaluating or preparing quotation in respect and/or evaluation of th e Purpose. B. Only to disclose the information and then only to the extent necessary to those of its employees to whom disclosure is necessary. C. Not to disclose the Confidential Information to any third party or the fact that the Confidential Information is b eing exchanged, except with the expressed authorisation in writing by the Disclosing Party and is obliged to enter into an identical confidentiality agreement with the such third party. **The Receiving Party can disclose information to the third party without the express authorization in writing by the Disclosing Party and not obliged to enter into an identical confidentiality agreement with such third party.** D. Not to copy or reduce the Confidential Information to writing or store in any computer readable form except as may be reasonably necessary for the Purpose. E. To return to the Disclosing Party on demand all information which has been supplied to or acquired by it in the form of drawings or written material or other recorded for m including all copies thereof, and to delete all information stored in computer readable form, and the Receiving Party will be responsible for the fulfilment of the above obligation on the part of its employees. CWF0 001 Mutual Non -Disclosure Agreement C.W. Fletcher & Sons Ltd
perturbation.explanation: By adding the sentence 'The Receiving Party can disclose information to the third party without the express authorization in writing by the Disclosing Party and not obliged to enter into an identical confidentiality agreement with such third party', creates an in-text contradiction with point C right before, where it states: 'Not to disclose the Confidential Information to any third party or the fact that the Confidential Information is b eing exchanged, except with the expressed authorisation in writing by the Disclosing Party and is obliged to enter into an identical confidentiality agreement with the such third party.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. In respect of all Confidential Information in whatever form acquired prior to or during the period of this Agreement by one party (the “Receiving Party”) from the other party (the “Disclosing Party”) relating to the Purpose, the Receiving Party undertakes: - A. Only to use the information for evaluating or preparing quotation in respect and/or evaluation of th e Purpose. B. Only to disclose the information and then only to the extent necessary to those of its employees to whom disclosure is necessary. C. Not to disclose the Confidential Information to any third party or the fact that the Confidential Information is b eing exchanged, except with the expressed authorisation in writing by the Disclosing Party and is obliged to enter into an identical confidentiality agreement with the such third party. The Receiving Party can disclose information to the third party without the express authorization in writing by the Disclosing Party and not obliged to enter into an identical confidentiality agreement with such third party. D. Not to copy or reduce the Confidential Information to writing or store in any computer readable form except as may be reasonably necessary for the Purpose. E. To return to the Disclosing Party on demand all information which has been supplied to or acquired by it in the form of drawings or written material or other recorded for m including all copies thereof, and to delete all information stored in computer readable form, and the Receiving Party will be responsible for the fulfilment of the above obligation on the part of its employees. CWF0 001 Mutual Non -Disclosure Agreement C.W. Fletcher & Sons Ltd
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text (which are the same in this case). The original and contradicted text states that the Receiving Party cannot disclose Confidential Information to any third party without express written authorization. The changed text adds a sentence explicitly allowing the Receiving Party to disclose information to a third party without express written authorization, creating a clear and direct contradiction. This aligns with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. The Parties agree that breach of this Agreement may result in a Party suffering irreparable harm from the unauthorised use or disclosure of the Confidential Information and may not have an adequate remedy in money or damage s. Accordingly, the Parties will be entitled to seek injunctive relief, and any other r elief permitted by applicable law, from any court of competent jurisdiction to enforce its rights under this agreement. Failure or delay by a Party to enforce any right, power, or privilege created in this Agreement will not operate as an implied waiver th ereof, nor will any single or partial enforcement preclude any other or further enforcement of any other right, power or privilege.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. The Parties agree that breach of this Agreement may result in a Party suffering irreparable harm from the unauthorised use or disclosure of the Confidential Information and may not have an adequate remedy in money or damage s. **However, the Parties agree that monetary damages will be the sole remedy for any breach of this agreement.** Accordingly, the Parties will be entitled to seek injunctive relief, and any other r elief permitted by applicable law, from any court of competent jurisdiction to enforce its rights under this agreement. Failure or delay by a Party to enforce any right, power, or privilege created in this Agreement will not operate as an implied waiver th ereof, nor will any single or partial enforcement preclude any other or further enforcement of any other right, power or privilege.
perturbation.explanation: The change introducing 'However, the Parties agree that monetary damages will be the sole remedy for any breach of this agreement' creates an in-text contradiction with the part of the section that states: 'Accordingly, the Parties will be entitled to seek injunctive relief, and any other r elief permitted by applicable law, from any court of competent jurisdiction to enforce its rights under this agreement.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. The Parties agree that breach of this Agreement may result in a Party suffering irreparable harm from the unauthorised use or disclosure of the Confidential Information and may not have an adequate remedy in money or damage s. However, the Parties agree that monetary damages will be the sole remedy for any breach of this agreement. Accordingly, the Parties will be entitled to seek injunctive relief, and any other r elief permitted by applicable law, from any court of competent jurisdiction to enforce its rights under this agreement. Failure or delay by a Party to enforce any right, power, or privilege created in this Agreement will not operate as an implied waiver th ereof, nor will any single or partial enforcement preclude any other or further enforcement of any other right, power or privilege.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating that 'monetary damages will be the sole remedy' while the original text, and the contradicted text, allow for 'injunctive relief' as a remedy. This creates a direct conflict because the changed text limits the available remedies, contradicting the possibility of seeking injunctive relief, which is permitted by the original agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 6. This Agreement supersedes all prior written or oral negotiations, commitments, agreement and understandings between the Parties relating to confidentiality and constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to confidentiality. The Agreement w ill not be deemed or construed to be modified, amended, rescinded, cancelled or waived, in whole or in part, except by a writing signed by the Parties’ authorised representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. This Agreement supersedes all prior written or oral negotiations, commitments, agreement and understandings between the Parties relating to confidentiality and constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to confidentiality. **The Parties can modify the agreement orally or in writing.**. The Agreement w ill not be deemed or construed to be modified, amended, rescinded, cancelled or waived, in whole or in part, except by a writing signed by the Parties’ authorised representatives.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the phrase 'The Parties can modify the agreement orally or in writing' to Section 6, creates an in-text contradiction with the second part of the sentence, which states: 'The Agreement w ill not be deemed or construed to be modified, amended, rescinded, cancelled or waived, in whole or in part, except by a writing signed by the Parties’ authorised representatives.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. This Agreement supersedes all prior written or oral negotiations, commitments, agreement and understandings between the Parties relating to confidentiality and constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to confidentiality. The Parties can modify the agreement orally or in writing.. The Agreement w ill not be deemed or construed to be modified, amended, rescinded, cancelled or waived, in whole or in part, except by a writing signed by the Parties’ authorised representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces the phrase 'The Parties can modify the agreement orally or in writing' into Section 6. This directly contradicts the subsequent statement in the same section, 'The Agreement w ill not be deemed or construed to be modified, amended, rescinded, cancelled or waived, in whole or in part, except by a writing signed by the Parties’ authorised representatives.' This is a strong, clear conflict regarding how the agreement can be modified, fitting the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: A. Only to use the information for evaluating or preparing quotation in respect and/or evaluation of th e Purpose. B. Only to disclose the information and then only to the extent necessary to those of its employees to whom disclosure is necessary.
perturbation.changed_text: A. Only to use the information for evaluating or preparing quotation in respect and/or evaluation of th e Purpose. B. Only to disclose the information and then only to the extent necessary to those of its employees to whom disclosure is necessary. **The receiving party may also use the information to engage in unrelated business ventures.**
perturbation.explanation: By adding the phrase 'The receiving party may also use the information to engage in unrelated business ventures' after point B, contradicts with the first part of Section 1, which says: 'In respect of all Confidential Information in whatever form acquired prior to or during the period of this Agreement by one party (the “Receiving Party”) from the other party (the “Disclosing Party”) relating to the Purpose, the Receiving Party undertakes'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. In respect of all Confidential Information in whatever form acquired prior to or during the period of this Agreement by one party (the “Receiving Party”) from the other party (the “Disclosing Party”) relating to the Purpose, the Receiving Party undertakes: - A. Only to use the information for evaluating or preparing quotation in respect and/or evaluation of th e Purpose. B. Only to disclose the information and then only to the extent necessary to those of its employees to whom disclosure is necessary. The receiving party may also use the information to engage in unrelated business ventures.
perturbation.justification: The added phrase 'The receiving party may also use the information to engage in unrelated business ventures' in the Changed Text directly contradicts the initial undertaking in Section 1 (Contradicted Text), which restricts the Receiving Party's use of Confidential Information to 'evaluating or preparing quotation in respect and/or evaluation of the Purpose'. The addition allows use outside the explicitly stated purpose, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.
